extractions.overview: Data Platform project, last updated 2026-02-25. Overall project status summary in progress. Accomplishments include completed API integration, resolved NextGenSIS access issues, deployed and validated incremental data load process, and completed archival stored procedures with email notifications. The project timeline has been extended by 1 week, with a new projected completion date of [Insert New Date].
extractions.stakeholders:
- Marcus Chen — Gained NextGenSIS access, confirmed it working, met to resolve access issues, familiarizing with NextGenSIS system, continuing data validation efforts, discussing anomalies, reconciling datasets, provided input for ReportHub API work, met to discuss placement strategy, paused affected data pipes
- Lisa Torres — Met to resolve NextGenSIS access issues
- Kevin Barnes — Met to resolve NextGenSIS access issues
- Carlos Yang — Met to resolve NextGenSIS access issues
- David Park — Validated incremental data load process, monitoring incremental data load for new streams
- Elena Kim — Completed archival stored procedure, implemented email notification for archived files, starting work on ReportHub API, will focus on PoC for automated data reconciliation with awards file
- Sarah Mitchell — Completed archival procedure (daily files for last 3 days, email notifications), has archival script ready for deployment, will begin work on FinAidSys
- Priya Sharma — Continuing data validation efforts, discussing anomalies, reconciling datasets
- Alex Petrov — Continuing data validation efforts, met to discuss placement strategy
- Rebecca Stone — Met to discuss placement strategy for Enrollment Services' developer code, successfully deployed code and ran tasks for data loading, will connect with James Wilson today to finalize remaining work
- Michael Hart — Successfully deployed code and ran tasks for data loading
- James Wilson — Implemented incremental load logic, will continue monitoring today, will work on loading data into HousingDB and dashboard development, will connect with Rebecca Stone today, will begin work on 'starters' (16 tables)
- Taylor Morgan — Reviewing responses from Registrar and Nathan Blake and Patricia Wells
- Nathan Blake — Provided input on swapping total credit fields
- Patricia Wells — Provided input on swapping total credit fields
- Amanda Fox — To begin loading data into HousingDB and then pick up dashboard development
- David Park — To work on 'starters' (16 tables)
- Rachel Lee — Provided input for FinAidSys work
extractions.timeline:
- 2026-02-25 — Last Updated
- Today (current status report date) — Registrar changes to datasets occurring in LegacySIS staging
- Today (current status report date) — Monitoring incremental load to ensure no column issues arise
- Today (current status report date) — James Wilson to connect with Rebecca Stone
- Today (current status report date) — Sarah Mitchell to begin work on FinAidSys
- In 1 week (from current status report date) — Marcus Chen will follow up on paused data pipes
- March — Validation activities for HousingDB data loading and dashboard development expected
extractions.risks:
- Security review pending
- Changes to 'check-in restriction messages' and 'registration restriction messages' datasets by the Registrar (from description text to code-only) occurring today in LegacySIS staging will break current DataCloud loading processes. Marcus Chen has paused the affected data pipes and will follow up in a week.
extractions.open_questions:
- Outreach to the overage office regarding a data anomaly (pending)
- David Park is monitoring the incremental data load for new streams to identify and resolve any column-related issues (pending)
- Taylor Morgan is still awaiting logic for another requested field (pending)
- Need to coordinate 'All Active' field change across approximately 140 reports (pending)
- Proof of Concept (PoC) for automated data reconciliation using DataCloud to call ReportHub API for data comparison, identifying missing or deleted records between LegacySIS and DataCloud (upcoming, Elena Kim to focus on awards file)
- Amanda Fox to begin loading data into HousingDB and then pick up dashboard development (upcoming)
- David Park to work on 'starters' (16 tables): verify auto-generated tasks, deploy scripts to load data into the raw layer, match counts between HousingDB and DataCloud, and provide Elena Kim with an audit list for row hash updates before final script deployment (upcoming)
- The data operations team will initiate a proof-of-concept for automated data reconciliation using DataCloud and the ReportHub API, focusing on smaller data files first (upcoming)
- James Wilson will connect with Rebecca Stone today to finalize remaining work related to code deployment and incremental loads (upcoming)
- James Wilson will begin work on the 'starters' (16 tables), verifying tasks, deploying scripts to load data into the raw layer, matching counts, and capturing ordered columns for Rebecca Stone's update procedure (upcoming)
- Sarah Mitchell has the archival script ready and will proceed with its deployment (upcoming)
- Sarah Mitchell will begin work on FinAidSys today, following input from Rachel Lee (upcoming)

conflicts.existing:
- Jaskaran Singh successfully obtained Workday access and is beginning to familiarize himself with the system.
- PERSON_9 completed the archival procedure, which includes archiving daily files for the last 3 days and setting up email notifications upon completion of the archival process.
The project is currently in an 'unmasking' phase, revealing accumulated technical debt previously hidden by manual spreadsheet patches. This phase is crucial for a successful NextGenSIS transition but has led to cascading logic discrepancies.
- A strategic shift has been initiated toward Workflow-Driven Discovery (NextGenSIS Pivot) to ensure a future-state model that is auditable and autonomous. This discovery phase is a current priority.
A strategic shift to 'Workflow-Driven Discovery' has been prioritized to build an auditable, autonomous future-state model.

conflicts.existing:
| R-NEW | Changes to 'check-in restriction messages' and 'registration restriction messages' datasets (changing from description to code-only output by GPE_5 today) will cause issues with existing Snowflake loading processes due to column order and field processing changes. | High | High | Pause affected Snowpipes, update loading processes, perform new bulk load when data is used. | Jaskaran Singh | Open |
| R-NEW | Overloading Informer production systems (e.g., registrations, term enrollment) if large data files are used for API testing | Medium | High | Use smaller files for PoC and explore staggered imports for larger data sets | PERSON_11 | Open |
| R-NEW | Inability to deliver 'hold codes' filter for registration may necessitate splitting the delivery into two different updates, delaying full functionality. | High | High | Obtain decision from PERSON_23 by tomorrow. | PERSON_2 | Open |
| R-NEW | Workday PII masking and other security aspects require further alignment with ORG_88 team, and their understanding of project discovery is currently limited. | Medium | High | Facilitate alignment and share discovery with ORG_88 team. | Sean | Open |
- Single Point of Failure: Project progress is critically tied to the specialized legacy knowledge of a single SME, creating an urgent resource risk (#3).
- Architectural Throughput: The Lead Architect is currently 'broad-fronted' across every active integration, acting as a bottleneck. Mitigation efforts include decentralizing technical leads for HousingDB and FinAidSys (#4, #6).
- Delay to 2027 NextGenSIS launch: Current prioritization of 100% legacy alignment and historical logic error fixing will delay the foundational build-out for the NextGenSIS launch.
- **Architectural Throughput**: Mitigation efforts now include decentralizing technical leads by pulling resources from HousingDB and FinAidSys to assist with intensive legacy data cleanup.
